My name is Cailee Lola Grayhorse-Pupecki and I am an artist and athlete who has a BFA in Fashion Design from the Academy of Art University.

I believe that in sports, and artistic movement you cannot be tense. When you are tense, failure strikes the body painfully. Failure should always be expected, so trying to be perfect is like trying to not be yourself. Everyday I challenge myself to envision how fashion design can allow bodies to be less tense, more confident and increasingly fluid in multitudes of environments. Giving somebody new clothes can give the gift of a brand-new perception. Garments can bless people with encouragement and bravery because it allows them to be the most transparent and truthful versions of themselves. My point of view is that if the mind maintains a strength unique to itself, the rest of the body's expression will follow.
I like hiking, and climbing the rocks
I love the climbing community

I’ve been working 12–14 hour days since I was 15 years old, balancing multiple jobs and building my path through discipline and passion. I’m a wardrobe stylist based in Los Angeles, a model, a graphic installer, and I also work in retail — each role shaping my work ethic and creativity. Fashion is my craft, but hard work is my foundation. Outside of work, sports have always been a huge part of my life. I grew up loving baseball, basketball, soccer, MMA, and boxing, and that competitive mindset carries into everything I do. I’m someone who believes in showing up, pushing through, and representing the people who hustle every day to chase bigger opportunities.
I'm born and raised in Kansas! I love animals and nature, and feel very comfortable in the outdoors.
I’ve done modeling and commercial work for over six years and have booked campaigns with some of the most top brands.
Open availability and ready to work. I always bring a positive attitude, strong work ethic, and great energy to every set.

Hi!!! I’m Olivia (love to be called Liv.) I am originally from the city of Chicago, but went to California for college because even though I’m a city girl, it has always been a dream of mine. I am a sophomore at San Diego State University studying Marketing. I began modeling in December 2025 and I love it! I enjoy learning from every photographer I work with and gaining more skills every shoot. I have an older sister who models too, who is my inspiration and best friend! Back home I have a black labradoodle named Finn who is basically my twin. Some of my favorite things include spending quality time with friends & family, the beach, long drives blasting music, and watching movies (I’m a fanatic and love going to the theater.) I also think Hamilton is the best musical ever made. My current food fixations are everything bagels and buttered noodles :). Those are some random facts about me, I appreciate your time and the opportunity!

Hi! I’m originally from India and grew up surrounded by vibrant culture—fashion, food, music, and traditions that continue to inspire me. I’m an engineer by background, but creativity has always been a big part of my life.

I’ve been a trained classical dancer for over 10 years, and I love using movement to tell stories and express emotion. Dance, fashion, and creative expression are where I feel most alive.

I care deeply about people and the communities around me. I strongly support neurodiversity, stand against colorism, and hope to use my platform and lived experiences to create more empathy and representation.

Fun fact: I can speak four native languages and I’m a huge chai lover (and yes… it’s just chai, not “chai tea”!).
I climb because I love taking on the challenge
I’m also Jamaican and Nigerian with runway experience

I’m eager to break the norm of models in today’s age. I would love to see more mixed race models in editorials and runway shows, as I believe it is a more accurate representation of what people are actually like today.

I’m very open and versatile, I work in the music business so I’m around and familiar with the Camera…selling a story and I have acted in a couple short films that I co-directed. I’m very athletic so any way I can show that I’d love as well!
I bring the energy and personality to every shoot, and I love expressing myself through my style and movement (my smiley piercing is just one way I do that).

I was born in Kathmandu, Nepal - half Tibetan and half Sherpa, adopted when I was 9 months old and grew up in Germany. I went to study my undergraduate degree in Innsbruck, Austria and am now pursuing an MA in Social Entrepreneurship and Change in Los Angeles. For a long time I did not like my unique facial features because people were either pointing it out or asking questions about my heritage. Once I fully accepted my uniqueness I was able to see it as my superpower and strength. The industry has changed and brought more diverse talent into it. I believe wholeheartedly that being different is something good and that more cultures should be included in the model industry.
I am also a professional dancer!

I have a Bachelor of Arts in Interdisciplinary Arts and Performance with a minor in Music, so I love anything to do with artistic expression! My greatest strength in dance is tap dance, and I choreograph a contemporary, storytelling version of it. I love to write poetry as well!
I am an LA based and have reliable transportation.

Hi! I’m Ajiri — a 24-year-old Nigerian-American creative based in Southern California. I’ve always gravitated toward visual storytelling and creative spaces, which has led me to explore modeling, content creation, and working behind the scenes on set.

I’ve done freelance modeling, production assistant work, and been an extra on a few sets, so I’m very comfortable on set and enjoy the collaborative process behind bringing ideas to life. I’m especially interested in projects that blend fashion, beauty, and culture in visually interesting ways.

Outside of that world, I work full-time as a Campaign Manager at a Gen Z influencer marketing agency where I help bring digital campaigns to life and collaborate with creators and brands. I’m also currently pursuing my Master of Public Health (MPH) remotely at NYU, so my days are usually a mix of creative work, strategy, and school.

As a Nigerian-American woman, navigating different cultural and creative spaces has shaped how I see the world and why I value representation, inclusivity, and community in the work I’m part of.

When I’m not working or studying, I love pouring into my various creative outlets — modeling, contributing to an independent magazine, creating content around fashion, beauty, and fragrance, and exploring new creative skills like coding and UX web design. I’m always excited to collaborate on projects that feel creative, expressive, and a little unexpected.

Hi! I’m Cara!  an actor, model. I  love storytelling in all its forms. I’ve worked across fashion, film, and editorial, including features in Vogue, FLAUNT, SCHÖN!, The Getty, and the Leica Gallery. I’m passionate about collaborating with creative teams to bring concepts and characters to life.

I am a native Texan and multi-hyphenate creative who moved to NYC 4 years ago. I am proud of where I come from and that I embody southern hospitality. I have around 50 and I’m always on the hunt to add more.

Thank you for any consideration, take care!

Nassia Matsa is a London-based, Athens-born model, with campaigns for brands such as Prada, Balenciaga, Dior, Burberry and Christian Louboutin as well as eleven editorials for Vogue titles worldwide.

Also she is a writer on culture and tech with published work in Financial Times, Wired and Vanity Fair amongst others.

With three nominations for the Model of the Year Awards by Models.com under the category of Social Mover for the year 2025, 2024 & 2021, Nassia has also collaborated with Mercedes-Benz to promote sustainable young designers and has given a TEDx talk on the importance of failure in order to succeed.

Nassia's viral article for Dazed has also advocated for AI ethics and regulation when it comes to fashion workers. She has also collaborated with Ivy League Cornell University on a study on the future of AI regulation & fashion, gave an interview to BBC News and spoke at London College of Fashion on the subject matter.

With two Master's degrees on media (from LSE and UAL), her creative skills are expanding across different outlets: from art direction for shoots for Farfetch & Burberry and Vogue Italia to radio producing for NTS Radio and hosting a Boiler Room event to creating visuals through digital media collaborating with brands such as Chanel, Miu Miu, Gucci & Loewe.

+Three nominations for the Model of the Year Awards by Models.com under the category of Social Mover for the year 2025, 2024 & 2021.
+Amongst the Best Fashion Instagram Accounts according to Vogue US three times.
+Amongst LFW'S Best Dressed Celebrities according to British Vogue.
